# Story 2.2: Seed initial `CHANGELOG.md` from existing git tags

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a downstream user pinning Skill Forge as a dependency,
I want a repo-resident `CHANGELOG.md` following Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 format with one versioned entry per published tag from `v0.3.0` through `v0.10.0`,
so that I can audit what changed in each past version without leaving the repo, the v1.0.0 release has a pre-existing changelog history to append to, and Story 3.1's `release.yaml` has a real seed file against which `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` can append the next release entry.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. The CHANGELOG seed SHALL be produced from `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0` (all-releases mode) — see Dev Notes § "CHANGELOG reconciliation decision" for the three reconciliation paths Story 2.1 flagged and the pre-condition that resolves the "Given no `CHANGELOG.md` exists" framing of the original AC.
2. The resulting `CHANGELOG.md` SHALL exist at the repo root and SHALL follow Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 shape: a top-level `# Changelog` header, an "All notable changes…" preamble line, then one `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD` versioned section per published tag from `v0.3.0` through `v0.10.0` (16 sections total — see Dev Notes § "Live-state recon" for the exact tag list), each containing one or more of the Keep-a-Changelog category headings (`### Added`, `### Changed`, `### Deprecated`, `### Removed`, `### Fixed`, `### Security`) as the conventionalcommits preset emits them.
3. Tags newer than the most recent published version (none currently — `v0.10.0` is HEAD's most recent tag) SHALL NOT have a section. An "Unreleased" section MAY appear at the top if the tool emits one for commits since `v0.10.0`; if it does, the `Unreleased` heading SHALL be left in place (it's the standard Keep-a-Changelog hook for accumulating un-tagged work; Story 3.1 will replace it on the next release cut).
4. The pre-existing hand-written `## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13` prose at the repo root SHALL NOT survive into the seeded `CHANGELOG.md`. The prose SHALL be preserved at `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (workshop file) so Story 5.1's pre-flight audit can pull the curated wording back when v1.0.0 is actually being cut. The workshop file SHALL include a one-line header explaining its purpose and pointing to Story 5.1 as the consumer.
5. The seed output SHALL be hand-reviewed for noise (commits incorrectly classified by the conventionalcommits preset, missing scopes, accidentally-shipped sentinel commits) and any corrections SHALL be applied directly to the file. AC #2 of the original epics.md formulation ("subsequent Epic 3 runs SHALL append new entries without overwriting existing prose") is satisfied by `conventional-changelog-cli -i ... -s`'s "stop at the first existing version heading" behavior — Story 3.1's append invocation will not touch the seeded prose, so hand-edits stick.
6. `README.md` SHALL gain a "Changelog" link pointing to `CHANGELOG.md`, placed adjacent to the existing "Release Process" subsection (lines 178-180 of README.md). The link SHALL be a one-sentence pointer ("Past releases are documented in [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md).") — not a badge, not a duplicated table of versions. Discoverability is the goal; not duplicating the changelog into the README.
7. `npm test` (the full quality suite — `test:schemas`, `test:install`, `test:cli`, `test:workflow`, `test:python`, `test:knowledge`, `validate:schemas`, `validate:skills`, `validate:refs`, `lint`, `lint:md`, `format:check`) SHALL pass on the resulting branch. In particular `lint:md` SHALL pass on the new `CHANGELOG.md` file — see Dev Notes § "Markdownlint regression surface" for likely fix patterns if the seeded output trips a rule.
8. No file outside the seven below SHALL be modified by this story: `CHANGELOG.md` (replaced), `README.md` (one-link addition), `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (new workshop file). No changes to `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `.github/workflows/*`, `docs/RELEASING.md`, `docs/STABILITY.md` (not yet authored — Story 2.3), or any source/test file. If the seeded file requires a markdownlint config tweak (e.g., extending an `ignores`/`MD024` rule to handle Keep-a-Changelog's repeated category headings), that tweak IS in scope and the touched config file is the eighth allowed file — see Dev Notes § "Markdownlint regression surface" for the expected fix path.
9. The commit SHALL use conventional-commit form. Suggested subject: `docs(changelog): seed initial CHANGELOG.md from existing tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0`. The body SHALL record (i) the reconciliation path chosen (recommended: Path 3 per Dev Notes § "CHANGELOG reconciliation decision") and the rationale, (ii) the exact `conventional-changelog-cli` invocation used (flags + version), (iii) one-line confirmation `npm test` passed post-seed, (iv) one-line note that the hand-written `[1.0.0]` prose was preserved to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` for Story 5.1, (v) one-line restatement that no `package.json` / `package-lock.json` / workflow changes piggybacked.
10. The deprecation warning Story 2.1 surfaced (`npm warn deprecated conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0: This package is no longer maintained.`) SHALL appear during this story's seed invocation as well — that is expected and NOT a blocker. The warning is registry-side; tool execution remains correct. Do NOT switch tools to silence it (the choice between staying on `conventional-changelog-cli@5` versus migrating to `conventional-changelog` versus `git-cliff` is a Story 3.1 design-phase decision, captured in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md` § "Story 3.1 tooling decision").

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] **Task 1 — Live-state recon** (Epic 1 retrospective carry-forward; informs every later task)
  - [ ] `git tag -l --sort=-creatordate` — confirm 16 tags `v0.10.0` → `v0.3.0` exist (no v0.1.x, no v0.2.x). If the count or shape differs from the recon snapshot in Dev Notes § "Live-state recon", capture the live state in Completion Notes and adjust expectations
  - [ ] `wc -c CHANGELOG.md` and `git log -1 --format='%H %ad' -- CHANGELOG.md` — confirm the file is still the 3148-byte hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft Story 2.1 left untouched. If a different shape is on disk (e.g. someone else seeded between Story 2.1 and now), STOP and reconcile out-of-band before proceeding
  - [ ] `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --version` — confirm the binary resolves locally (Story 2.1's lockfile carries `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0`); expected output `5.0.0`
  - [ ] `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help | head -40` — re-read the flag surface fresh. AC #1 mandates `-r 0`; the greenfield form needs `-o CHANGELOG.md` (write to a fresh outfile) or stdout-redirect, NOT `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` (which is the append mode Story 3.1 uses against an existing seed). Confirm flag semantics before invocation in Task 4
  - [ ] `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --oneline | wc -l` — record the count of un-tagged commits since the most recent release. The seed run with `-r 0` will emit an `## Unreleased` section (or omit it if zero commits since last tag) — count expectation feeds AC #3 verification

- [ ] **Task 2 — Reconciliation decision (Story 2.2's owner choice)** (AC #1, AC #4)
  - [ ] Read Dev Notes § "CHANGELOG reconciliation decision" in this story file in full — three paths are enumerated verbatim per the epics.md § Story 2.2 header carry-forward, plus a recommended path (Path 3) with reasoning
  - [ ] Pick one of the three paths. The recommendation is **Path 3** (preserve existing 1.0.0 prose to a workshop file, delete `CHANGELOG.md`, seed greenfield from tags, defer hand-curated v1.0.0 entry to Story 5.1's pre-flight). If choosing Path 1 or Path 2, capture the rationale in this story's Dev Agent Record § "Completion Notes List" before any mutation — divergence from the recommendation is allowed but MUST be justified in writing for traceability into Story 3.1 / 5.1
  - [ ] Whichever path is chosen, the commit body (AC #9 (i)) MUST name it explicitly so future readers can trace the decision

- [ ] **Task 3 — Preserve existing hand-written v1.0.0 prose** (AC #4) [if Path 3 chosen]
  - [ ] `mkdir -p _bmad-output/planning-artifacts` (idempotent — directory already exists per planning-artifacts layout)
  - [ ] Copy the entire current `CHANGELOG.md` contents into `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md`, prepending a header block that explains: (a) what this file is — the hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft preserved during Story 2.2's CHANGELOG seed, (b) why it's here — Story 5.1's pre-flight audit will pull from it when authoring the actual v1.0.0 CHANGELOG entry, (c) the source — moved from `CHANGELOG.md` repo root by Story 2.2's reconciliation Path 3
  - [ ] Verify the workshop file's content matches the original byte-for-byte except for the prepended header: `diff <(tail -n +N _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md) CHANGELOG.md` (where N is the line count of the prepended header + 1) should produce no output
  - [ ] [Path 1 alternative] If Path 1 chosen instead, rename `CHANGELOG.md` → `CHANGELOG.draft.md` (or fold into a section in `docs/STABILITY.md` if Story 2.3 has authored it — but it hasn't yet, so this sub-option is not currently viable)
  - [ ] [Path 2 alternative] If Path 2 chosen instead, skip this task entirely and skip Task 4's deletion sub-step. Task 5's invocation must then be `-i CHANGELOG.md -s -r 0` (append-mode-against-existing) and the dev agent MUST verify by inspection that the existing `[1.0.0]` heading does not cause the tool to silently skip generating entries for tags older than 1.0.0

- [ ] **Task 4 — Clear the way for greenfield seed** (AC #1) [if Path 3 chosen]
  - [ ] `rm CHANGELOG.md` — staged via Task 7's `git add CHANGELOG.md` (which will register a delete + re-add as a single replacement in the commit)
  - [ ] Confirm `ls CHANGELOG.md 2>&1` reports "No such file" before Task 5 runs (Task 5's `-o CHANGELOG.md` form expects a non-existent target file in a clean greenfield setup)

- [ ] **Task 5 — Run the seed** (AC #1, AC #2, AC #3)
  - [ ] Invocation: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md`. Exact rationale for each flag:
    - `-p conventionalcommits` — the modern preset (transitively resolved via Story 2.1's `conventional-changelog@^6.0.0`); produces Keep-a-Changelog category headings (Added/Changed/Fixed/etc.) keyed off conventional-commit type prefixes (feat/fix/perf/refactor/etc.)
    - `-r 0` — emit one section per release-tag in history; `0` means "all releases" (default is `1` which emits only the latest). Mandatory per AC #1
    - `-o CHANGELOG.md` — write to a new outfile. Use this form (not `-i ... -s`) when seeding greenfield. `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` is the append-mode invocation Story 3.1 will use AGAINST the seeded file, NOT for the seed itself
  - [ ] Capture stdout/stderr to a transient log: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log`. The log will show the deprecation warning (expected per AC #10) and any preset-loading or git-walking errors. Discard the log after committing the file (do not commit `/tmp` content)
  - [ ] `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` — sanity check the seed produced something. Expect dozens-to-hundreds of lines (16 tag sections × variable per-tag commit counts, plus preamble). An output under ~30 lines indicates the preset failed to walk history — re-investigate before proceeding
  - [ ] `grep -c "^## " CHANGELOG.md` — count level-2 headings. Expect 16 (one per tag from v0.3.0 through v0.10.0) plus 0-or-1 for an `## [Unreleased]` header. If significantly fewer, the `-r 0` flag did not take — re-check the invocation

- [ ] **Task 6 — Hand-review and clean up** (AC #2, AC #5, AC #7)
  - [ ] Open the seeded `CHANGELOG.md` and scan top-to-bottom. Expected issues to look for and resolve:
    - **Missing top-level preamble**: the conventionalcommits preset typically emits only `# Changelog` and the version sections. Add the standard Keep-a-Changelog preamble line ("All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).") below the `# Changelog` heading and before the first `## [...]` section. Match the style the existing 2026-04-13 hand-written file used (per the 3148-byte preserved copy)
    - **Misclassified commits**: pre-v0.6.0 commits may have non-conventional prefixes ("Add Entire CLI setup", "0.3.0", "Initial commit"); the preset will either skip them entirely or lump them under "Other". If a tag's section is empty (just a date heading with no entries), that's expected for early tags and acceptable — leave the empty section to preserve the version chronology. AC #2 explicitly permits hand-editing
    - **Compare-link footers**: the preset emits `[unreleased]: https://...` and `[X.Y.Z]: https://...` link-reference footers at the bottom. Verify they target `armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` (not a fallback URL pulled from `package.json`'s `repository` field if it differs). The `repository.url` in `package.json` is `git+https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.git` — the preset SHOULD normalize this correctly, but verify the first compare link by hand
    - **Date format consistency**: each `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD` heading uses the tag's commit date. Spot-check three dates against `git log -1 --format=%ad --date=short v<X.Y.Z>` to confirm
  - [ ] If hand-edits are made, capture the edit list in Completion Notes (file:line + rationale). AC #2's intent is that subsequent Story 3.1 runs will not overwrite these edits — verifying that intent is Story 3.2's alpha-cut job, not this story's
  - [ ] Run `npm run lint:md` — confirm `CHANGELOG.md` passes markdownlint. If it doesn't, see Dev Notes § "Markdownlint regression surface" for the expected fix patterns. Do NOT add `CHANGELOG.md` to a markdownlint ignore list — fix the file or extend a rule scoped to Keep-a-Changelog conventions

- [ ] **Task 7 — Add README link** (AC #6)
  - [ ] Open `README.md`. Locate the existing `## Release Process` section (line 178). Insert a new `## Changelog` section directly above it (line 178 → becomes a Changelog section heading, then the existing Release Process drops to ~line 182): `## Changelog\n\nPast releases are documented in [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md).\n\n` — three lines plus blank-line padding for markdownlint
  - [ ] Verify the README's existing "Learn More" / "Acknowledgements" / "Release Process" / "License" / "Contributing" ordering is preserved. The new section sits between Contributing (line 174) and Release Process (line 178) by section-flow logic (changelog is reader-facing project history; release process is maintainer-facing operational doc)
  - [ ] Run `npm run lint:md` again — confirm `README.md` still passes after the insertion. Expected to pass since the format mirrors the surrounding sections

- [ ] **Task 8 — Full quality suite regression gate** (AC #7)
  - [ ] Run `npm test`. Expect all 12 subcommands green. Particularly:
    - `lint:md` MUST pass on the new `CHANGELOG.md` and modified `README.md`
    - `format:check` SHOULD pass on the new file (markdown isn't in Prettier's default scope per `package.json` line 41 — `*.{js,cjs,mjs,json,yaml}` — so the new `.md` file is invisible to it, no action needed)
    - `validate:refs` SHALL pass — the README's new `[CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)` link is a relative reference that should resolve cleanly (the existing reference validator at `tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict` walks all relative links in README.md per `npm test`'s suite)
  - [ ] If `lint:md` trips on the new `CHANGELOG.md`, narrow the cause. Common Keep-a-Changelog vs markdownlint frictions:
    - **MD024** (no-duplicate-heading) — Keep-a-Changelog's `### Added` heading repeats across every version section; the standard fix is `siblings_only: true` in the rule config (allow duplicate headings if they're not at the same level under the same parent). Check if `.markdownlint*.json` / `.markdownlint*.yaml` already configures this; if not, add the narrowest possible scope (CHANGELOG.md only) per § "Markdownlint regression surface" patterns
    - **MD013** (line-length) — long URLs in compare-link footers may exceed 120-char default. Configure `code_blocks: false, tables: false, heading_line_length: 999` or scope a CHANGELOG-only override
    - **MD041** (first-line-h1) — should pass since the preset emits `# Changelog` first
    - **MD025** (single-h1) — should pass for same reason

- [ ] **Task 9 — Stage and commit** (AC #8, AC #9)
  - [ ] `git status --short` — confirm only the expected files: `M README.md`, `D CHANGELOG.md` + `?? CHANGELOG.md` (or just `M CHANGELOG.md` depending on git's rename detection), `?? _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md`. If `package.json` / `package-lock.json` / `.github/workflows/` show changes, STOP — that violates AC #8
  - [ ] Stage the three expected files explicitly: `git add CHANGELOG.md README.md _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (and any narrowest-scope markdownlint config tweak from Task 8 — capture which file in commit body if applicable)
  - [ ] `git diff --cached --stat` — confirm exactly the expected files. If anything else appears, investigate before committing
  - [ ] Commit with conventional form. Suggested subject: `docs(changelog): seed initial CHANGELOG.md from existing tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0`. Body MUST capture the five elements AC #9 enumerates: (i) reconciliation path + rationale, (ii) exact `conventional-changelog-cli` invocation + version, (iii) `npm test` result, (iv) v1.0.0 prose preservation note, (v) "no package.json / lockfile / workflow piggyback" restatement

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

This is a **one-shot seed of `CHANGELOG.md` from git tag history** — a single `npx conventional-changelog-cli` invocation, a hand-review pass, and a one-link addition to `README.md`. The repo diff is two-to-four files: `CHANGELOG.md` (replaced from greenfield), `README.md` (+3 lines), `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (new workshop file preserving the existing 1.0.0 prose for Story 5.1), and optionally one markdownlint config file (only if the seeded file's MD024-duplicate-heading pattern requires a narrowed config). No tooling, no scripts, no workflows, no STABILITY.md.

The work product is a real, repo-resident, Keep-a-Changelog-shaped historical record from `v0.3.0` through `v0.10.0` that downstream consumers can audit without leaving the repo, and that Story 3.1's `release.yaml` can append to via `npx conventional-changelog-cli -i CHANGELOG.md -s` on every future release.

**Scope discipline — what NOT to do here:**

- Do NOT preserve the pre-existing hand-written `## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13` entry inside the seeded `CHANGELOG.md`. It pre-announces a version that has not been released (current published version is `0.10.0`). Letting it survive into the seeded file means downstream readers see a "v1.0.0 — released 2026-04-13" entry that contradicts npm. The prose ITSELF is valuable raw material for Story 5.1's eventual hand-curated v1.0.0 entry — preserve it to a workshop file, not into the published changelog.
- Do NOT author `docs/STABILITY.md`. That is Story 2.3's scope. Story 2.2 ships changelog history; STABILITY.md ships the v1.0.0 API contract. Two orthogonal artifacts.
- Do NOT extend `docs/RELEASING.md` with rollback playbook content. That is Story 4.1's scope. The existing `<!-- Rollback Playbook — added in Story 4.1 -->` placeholder in `docs/RELEASING.md` (per Epic 1 retro § "Patterns across all three stories") MUST stay intact.
- Do NOT add `release:changelog` or any `release:*` npm scripts to `package.json`. Story 4.2 will deprecate the existing `release:*` scripts; Story 6.2 will delete them. Adding new ones now creates a second canonical path that the deprecation/deletion stories then have to retire — exactly the trap Story 2.1's scope discipline (per its Dev Notes § "Scope discipline — what NOT to do here") preempted.
- Do NOT switch tools to silence the `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0` deprecation warning. The choice between staying on the deprecated-but-working `conventional-changelog-cli@5` versus migrating to the suggested `conventional-changelog` package versus the rejected-but-still-viable `git-cliff` is captured in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md` § "Story 3.1 tooling decision". Forcing the decision here pre-empts Story 3.1's design phase.
- Do NOT bump `conventional-changelog-cli` past `^5.0.0` in `package.json`. Story 2.1 deliberately pinned `^5.0.0`. Bumping requires a separate deliberate change with its own `npm test` regression gate.
- Do NOT add `CHANGELOG.md` to a markdownlint ignore list as a shortcut around MD024 (duplicate-heading). The right fix is a narrow rule config (sibling-only duplicates allowed) or a CHANGELOG-scoped override — not exempting the file from quality gates.
- Do NOT manually copy commit text into the seeded file. The whole point of the tool is deterministic categorization; hand-fabricating entries defeats the audit-trail-from-git semantic Story 3.1 will rely on. Hand EDITS for noise correction are fine (AC #2); hand AUTHORING new entries is not.

### Live-state recon — as of 2026-04-21

**Tag history (16 tags, all matching `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`):**

```
v0.10.0  v0.9.0  v0.8.4  v0.8.3  v0.8.2  v0.8.1  v0.8.0  v0.7.4
v0.7.3   v0.7.2  v0.7.1  v0.7.0  v0.6.0  v0.5.0  v0.4.0  v0.3.0
```

No `v0.1.x`, no `v0.2.x`, no prerelease tags (`-rc.N` / `-alpha.N` / `-beta.N`). First tag `v0.3.0` was created at commit `f81b975` ("0.3.0"). Pre-`v0.3.0` history is 3 commits (`Initial commit`, `Add Entire CLI setup and integrate hooks with Husky`, `chore: track package-lock.json for reproducible installs`) plus the v0.3.0 commit itself — none of these will produce categorized entries since the first two predate conventional-commit discipline.

### CHANGELOG reconciliation decision

Per the epics.md Story 2.2 § header (lines 291-297), **three paths are available** for resolving the conflict between the original AC #1's "Given no `CHANGELOG.md` exists" framing and the live-state reality (a hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft already at the repo root). Story 2.1's Dev Notes § "Existing CHANGELOG.md — flag for Story 2.2" is the verbatim source for these three paths. **Decision is Story 2.2's owner to make**, but the reasoning below makes Path 3 the strongly recommended default:

**Path 1 — Rename existing file, seed greenfield, hand-merge prose later**

- Operation: `git mv CHANGELOG.md CHANGELOG.draft.md` (or fold into `docs/STABILITY.md`'s launch section), then `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md`, then hand-merge the 1.0.0 prose into the seeded file's top entry
- Tradeoffs:
  - PRO: Preserves the prose file in-tree where reviewers see it
  - CON: `CHANGELOG.draft.md` is non-standard naming (downstream tools / IDEs that auto-detect changelogs by canonical filename will not find it). `docs/STABILITY.md` does not yet exist (Story 2.3's job) and folding the changelog prose into a file about API contracts mixes two orthogonal concerns
  - CON: Hand-merging the 1.0.0 prose into the seeded file's top entry presumes 1.0.0 is the latest version — which it isn't. 1.0.0 has not been released

**Path 2 — Keep existing file, run `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` in append mode**

- Operation: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s -r 0` against the existing 3148-byte file
- Tradeoffs:
  - PRO: Single-file diff (only `CHANGELOG.md` changes); no workshop file needed
  - CON: `-i ... -s` semantics: the tool reads the existing file, finds the most recent version heading (`## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13`), and inserts new entries ABOVE that heading. With `-r 0`, it would walk all releases, but the existing `[1.0.0]` heading is in the way — the tool's "stop at the most recent existing version" heuristic may either (a) skip emitting any sections older than 1.0.0 (because 1.0.0 is the apparent boundary), (b) emit them above 1.0.0 producing a chronologically-inverted file, or (c) just refuse to walk past 1.0.0. Behavior is uncertain enough that this path requires a probe-then-decide loop the dev agent has not budgeted for
  - CON: Even if the tool produces correct output, the resulting file still contains the pre-emptive `[1.0.0]` entry that contradicts npm reality. AC #2 / AC #4 then have nowhere to land cleanly

**Path 3 — Delete pre-emptive `[1.0.0]` entry, seed from tags, defer hand-curated 1.0.0 to Story 5.1 (RECOMMENDED)**

- Operation: copy current CHANGELOG.md to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (workshop file with header), `rm CHANGELOG.md`, run `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md` against an empty target, hand-review for noise, commit
- Tradeoffs:
  - PRO: Aligns with epics.md Story 5.1 AC ("`CHANGELOG.md` exists in repo with a hand-curated v1.0.0 entry added at the top (not just auto-generated; curator's judgment on wording, not commit grep)") — Story 5.1 explicitly anticipates authoring the v1.0.0 entry as part of pre-flight audit
  - PRO: Aligns with original epics.md AC #1's "Given no `CHANGELOG.md` exists" framing (we genuinely delete first, so the file doesn't exist when the seed runs)
  - PRO: Workshop file `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` is a discoverable artifact that Story 5.1's `create-story` will surface. The 14-workflow table, 23-IDE list, and "Highlights" prose are not lost — just staged for the moment they actually correspond to a real published version
  - PRO: Resulting `CHANGELOG.md` is unambiguous — every entry corresponds to a real published tag on npm
  - CON: Two-file diff (CHANGELOG.md + workshop file) instead of one. Minor cost
  - CON: Story 5.1's dev agent must remember to pull from the workshop file. Mitigated by referencing it explicitly in the workshop file's header AND in this story's Completion Notes (so when create-story runs for Story 5.1, the workshop file's existence is in the immediately-prior story's Dev Notes)

**Recommendation: Path 3.** It cleanly resolves the AC contradiction, preserves the 1.0.0 prose for Story 5.1's planned use, and produces a published `CHANGELOG.md` whose every entry corresponds to a real npm-published version.

If choosing Path 1 or Path 2 instead, the Completion Notes MUST capture the rationale and the Tasks section MUST be adapted (Path 1 swaps Task 4's `rm` for `git mv`; Path 2 skips Tasks 3-4 entirely and reshapes Task 5's invocation to `-i ... -s`).

### Tool flag reference — for the dev agent

`conventional-changelog-cli` flag semantics (per Story 2.1 verification + the tool's `--help` surface):

| Flag | Meaning | Story 2.2 (seed) | Story 3.1 (append) |
|------|---------|------------------|---------------------|
| `-p <preset>` | Conventional commit preset | `conventionalcommits` | `conventionalcommits` |
| `-r <n>` | Release count: `0`=all, `1`=latest only (default) | `0` (per AC #1) | `1` (default; new entry only) |
| `-o <file>` | Outfile (write to fresh file) | `CHANGELOG.md` (greenfield) | not used |
| `-i <file>` | Infile (read existing) | not used (greenfield) | `CHANGELOG.md` |
| `-s` | Same-file (overwrite infile) | not used | yes (paired with `-i`) |

**Critical distinction:** `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` is APPEND mode (used by Story 3.1's `release.yaml` for incremental updates). It expects an existing file with prior version headings. Using it for a greenfield seed against a deleted-or-empty file is undefined behavior. Story 2.2's seed MUST use `-o CHANGELOG.md` (write to a fresh outfile) instead.

If the dev agent's `--help` output disagrees with this table at execution time (the tool's surface may have shifted between the recon snapshot and execution), trust `--help` over the table and capture the deviation in Completion Notes.

### Markdownlint regression surface

`markdownlint-cli2` (`npm run lint:md`) walks every `**/*.md` file in the repo per `package.json` line 45. The new `CHANGELOG.md` will be linted; the existing 2026-04-13 hand-written file passed (per Story 2.1 Task 5 green run) but the seeded version's shape will differ. Likely friction points:

1. **MD024 (no-duplicate-heading)** — Keep-a-Changelog repeats `### Added`, `### Fixed`, `### Changed` etc. across every version section. Default markdownlint config flags these as duplicates. Standard fix: enable `siblings_only: true` for MD024 (allow duplicate headings if they're under different parent headings — which `### Added` always is, since each appears under a different `## [X.Y.Z]` parent). Check the existing markdownlint config first (`.markdownlint*.json`, `.markdownlint*.yaml`, `package.json` `markdownlint` key) to see if `siblings_only` is already configured. If not, add the narrowest possible config — ideally CHANGELOG-scoped via a `.markdownlint-cli2.yaml` glob override, not a repo-wide change

2. **MD013 (line-length)** — long URLs in compare-link footers (`[v0.10.0]: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v0.9.0...v0.10.0`) may exceed 120-char default. Fix: scoped override allowing long lines in the link-reference footer area, or globally `tables: false, code_blocks: false` if not already set

3. **MD041 (first-line-h1)** — should pass; the conventionalcommits preset emits `# Changelog` as the first heading

4. **MD025 (single-h1)** — should pass; only one `# Changelog` heading, all version sections are `## [X.Y.Z]`

5. **MD012 (no-multiple-blanks)** — preset output may have trailing/leading blanks between sections. Hand-edit if flagged

If any rule trips, prefer narrowing the rule's scope over disabling it. Adding `CHANGELOG.md` to an `ignores` list is forbidden (per Dev Notes § "Scope discipline" above) — quality gates apply to repo-resident files.

### Coupling with past, current, and future stories

- **Story 1.x (done)** — orthogonal. Branch protection + release env + npm TP are release-security infrastructure; Story 2.2 is content authoring. No direct coupling
- **Story 2.1 (done, commit `2e97830`)** — DIRECT precondition. Story 2.1 added `conventional-changelog-cli@^5.0.0` as a devDep so Story 2.2 can invoke `npx conventional-changelog-cli` without a network round-trip. Story 2.1 also explicitly left `CHANGELOG.md` untouched (its AC #6) to give Story 2.2 a clean decision surface. Story 2.1's Dev Notes § "Existing CHANGELOG.md — flag for Story 2.2" was carried into epics.md Story 2.2 § header on 2026-04-21 (per Story 2.1 Review Findings § first patch entry) — that carry-forward is the source of the three reconciliation paths above
- **Story 2.3 (backlog, sibling)** — orthogonal. STABILITY.md is the public API contract; CHANGELOG.md is release history. Both ship before v1.0.0 cut. Story 2.2 does NOT touch STABILITY.md and does NOT presume Story 2.3 has run
- **Story 3.1 (backlog, downstream consumer)** — DIRECT downstream consumer. `release.yaml`'s CHANGELOG-update phase invokes `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` (per epics.md Story 3.1 § "[CHANGELOG update]" + research doc line 773). That invocation REQUIRES the seeded file Story 2.2 produces — without it, the `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` form has nothing to read. Story 3.1's design phase (per `deferred-work.md` § "Story 3.1 tooling decision") may also revisit the tooling choice (`conventional-changelog-cli@5` vs `conventional-changelog` vs `git-cliff`); that decision is OUT OF SCOPE for Story 2.2

The workshop file's header MUST reference Story 5.1 as the consumer so create-story for 5.1 surfaces it

content.content:
Generate a changelog from git metadata.

  Usage
    conventional-changelog

  Example
    conventional-changelog -i CHANGELOG.md --same-file

  Options
    -i, --infile              Read the CHANGELOG from this file

    -o, --outfile             Write the CHANGELOG to this file
                              If unspecified, it prints to stdout

    -s, --same-file           Outputting to the infile so you don't need to specify the same file as outfile

    -p, --preset              Name of the preset you want to use. Must be one of the following:
                              angular, atom, codemirror, conventionalcommits, ember, eslint, express, jquery or jshint

    -k, --pkg                 A filepath of where your package.json is located
                              Default is the closest package.json from cwd

    -a, --append              Should the newer release be appended to the older release
                              Default: false

    -r, --release-count       How many releases to be generated from the latest
                              If 0, the whole changelog will be regenerated and the outfile will be overwritten
                              Default: 1

    --skip-unstable           If given, unstable tags will be skipped, e.g., x.x.x-alpha.1, x.x.x-rc.2

    -u, --output-unreleased   Output unreleased changelog

    -v, --verbose             Verbose output. Use this for debugging
                              Default: false

    -n, --config              A filepath of your config script
                              Example of a config script: https://github.com/conventional-changelog/conventional-changelog/blob/master/packages/conventional-changelog-cli/test/fixtures/config.cjs

    -c, --context             A filepath of a json that is used to define template variables
    -l, --lerna-package       Generate a changelog for a specific lerna package (:pkg-name@1.0.0)
    -t, --tag-prefix          Tag prefix to consider when reading the tags
    --commit-path             Generate a changelog scoped to a specific directory

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
4	
5	## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13
6	
7	### First Major Release
8	
9	First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.
10	
11	### Initial Release
12	
13	Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.
14	
15	### Highlights
16	
17	- **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
18	- **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
19	- **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
20	- **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
21	- **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
22	- **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
23	- **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows
24	
25	### Workflows
26	
27	| Trigger | Name | Purpose |
28	|---------|------|---------|
29	| SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
30	| AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
31	| BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
32	| CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
33	| QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
34	| SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
35	| US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
36	| AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
37	| TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
38	| VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
39	| RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
40	| EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
41	| RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
42	| DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |
43	
44	### Confidence Tiers
45	
46	- **T1** — AST-verified signatures (Forge/Forge+/Deep)
47	- **T1-low** — Source reading without structural verification (Quick)
48	- **T2** — QMD-enriched temporal context (Deep)
49	- **T3** — External documentation, quarantined as untrusted
50	
51	### IDE Support
52	
53	23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev
54	
55	### Links

57	- Documentation: <https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge>
58	- npm: <https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge>

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13

### First Major Release

First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.

### Initial Release

Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.

### Highlights

- **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
- **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
- **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
- **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
- **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
- **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
- **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows

### Workflows

| Trigger | Name | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
| AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
| BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
| CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
| QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
| SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
| US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
| AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
| TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
| VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
| RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
| EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
| RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
| DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |

### Confidence Tiers

- **T1** — AST-verified signatures (Forge/Forge+/Deep)
- **T1-low** — Source reading without structural verification (Quick)
- **T2** — QMD-enriched temporal context (Deep)
- **T3** — External documentation, quarantined as untrusted

### IDE Support

23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev

### Links

- Documentation: <https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge>
- npm: <https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge>

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13

### First Major Release

First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.

### Initial Release

Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.

### Highlights

- **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
- **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
- **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
- **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
- **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
- **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
- **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows

### Workflows

| Trigger | Name | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
| AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
| BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
| CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
| QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
| SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
| US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
| AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
| TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
| VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
| RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
| EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
| RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
| DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |

### Confidence Tiers

- **T1** — AST-verified signatures (Forge/Forge+/Deep)
- **T1-low** — Source reading without structural verification (Quick)
- **T2** — QMD-enriched temporal context (Deep)

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13

### First Major Release

First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.

### Initial Release

Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.

### Highlights

- **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
- **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
- **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
- **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
- **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
- **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
- **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows

### Workflows

| Trigger | Name | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
| AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
| BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
| CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
| QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
| SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
| US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
| AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
| TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
| VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
| RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
| EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
| RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
| DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |

### Confidence Tiers

- **T1** — AST-verified signatures (Forge/Forge+/Deep)
- **T1-low** — Source reading without structural verification (Quick)
- **T2** — QMD-enriched temporal context (Deep)
- **T3** — External documentation, quarantined as untrusted

### IDE Support

23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev

### Links

- Documentation: <https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge>
- npm: <https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge>

content.text:
Both `_bmad/` and `_bmad-output/` are gitignored repo-wide. The workshop file stays local-only (consistent with other `_bmad-output/` artifacts like `epics.md`). This satisfies AC #8 ("modified"), and the commit body will reference the workshop file's local preservation per AC #9 (iv).

Let me now update the story file with Dev Agent Record, mark tasks [x], and set Status to review.

input.old_string:
- [ ] **Task 1 — Live-state recon** (Epic 1 retrospective carry-forward; informs every later task)
  - [ ] `git tag -l --sort=-creatordate` — confirm 16 tags `v0.10.0` → `v0.3.0` exist (no v0.1.x, no v0.2.x). If the count or shape differs from the recon snapshot in Dev Notes § "Live-state recon", capture the live state in Completion Notes and adjust expectations
  - [ ] `wc -c CHANGELOG.md` and `git log -1 --format='%H %ad' -- CHANGELOG.md` — confirm the file is still the 3148-byte hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft Story 2.1 left untouched. If a different shape is on disk (e.g. someone else seeded between Story 2.1 and now), STOP and reconcile out-of-band before proceeding
  - [ ] `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --version` — confirm the binary resolves locally (Story 2.1's lockfile carries `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0`); expected output `5.0.0`
  - [ ] `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help | head -40` — re-read the flag surface fresh. AC #1 mandates `-r 0`; the greenfield form needs `-o CHANGELOG.md` (write to a fresh outfile) or stdout-redirect, NOT `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` (which is the append mode Story 3.1 uses against an existing seed). Confirm flag semantics before invocation in Task 4
  - [ ] `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --oneline | wc -l` — record the count of un-tagged commits since the most recent release. The seed run with `-r 0` will emit an `## Unreleased` section (or omit it if zero commits since last tag) — count expectation feeds AC #3 verification

- [ ] **Task 2 — Reconciliation decision (Story 2.2's owner choice)** (AC #1, AC #4)
  - [ ] Read Dev Notes § "CHANGELOG reconciliation decision" in this story file in full — three paths are enumerated verbatim per the epics.md § Story 2.2 header carry-forward, plus a recommended path (Path 3) with reasoning
  - [ ] Pick one of the three paths. The recommendation is **Path 3** (preserve existing 1.0.0 prose to a workshop file, delete `CHANGELOG.md`, seed greenfield from tags, defer hand-curated v1.0.0 entry to Story 5.1's pre-flight). If choosing Path 1 or Path 2, capture the rationale in this story's Dev Agent Record § "Completion Notes List" before any mutation — divergence from the recommendation is allowed but MUST be justified in writing for traceability into Story 3.1 / 5.1
  - [ ] Whichever path is chosen, the commit body (AC #9 (i)) MUST name it explicitly so future readers can trace the decision

- [ ] **Task 3 — Preserve existing hand-written v1.0.0 prose** (AC #4) [if Path 3 chosen]
  - [ ] `mkdir -p _bmad-output/planning-artifacts` (idempotent — directory already exists per planning-artifacts layout)
  - [ ] Copy the entire current `CHANGELOG.md` contents into `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md`, prepending a header block that explains: (a) what this file is — the hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft preserved during Story 2.2's CHANGELOG seed, (b) why it's here — Story 5.1's pre-flight audit will pull from it when authoring the actual v1.0.0 CHANGELOG entry, (c) the source — moved from `CHANGELOG.md` repo root by Story 2.2's reconciliation Path 3
  - [ ] Verify the workshop file's content matches the original byte-for-byte except for the prepended header: `diff <(tail -n +N _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md) CHANGELOG.md` (where N is the line count of the prepended header + 1) should produce no output
  - [ ] [Path 1 alternative] If Path 1 chosen instead, rename `CHANGELOG.md` → `CHANGELOG.draft.md` (or fold into a section in `docs/STABILITY.md` if Story 2.3 has authored it — but it hasn't yet, so this sub-option is not currently viable)
  - [ ] [Path 2 alternative] If Path 2 chosen instead, skip this task entirely and skip Task 4's deletion sub-step. Task 5's invocation must then be `-i CHANGELOG.md -s -r 0` (append-mode-against-existing) and the dev agent MUST verify by inspection that the existing `[1.0.0]` heading does not cause the tool to silently skip generating entries for tags older than 1.0.0

- [ ] **Task 4 — Clear the way for greenfield seed** (AC #1) [if Path 3 chosen]
  - [ ] `rm CHANGELOG.md` — staged via Task 7's `git add CHANGELOG.md` (which will register a delete + re-add as a single replacement in the commit)
  - [ ] Confirm `ls CHANGELOG.md 2>&1` reports "No such file" before Task 5 runs (Task 5's `-o CHANGELOG.md` form expects a non-existent target file in a clean greenfield setup)

- [ ] **Task 5 — Run the seed** (AC #1, AC #2, AC #3)
  - [ ] Invocation: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md`. Exact rationale for each flag:
    - `-p conventionalcommits` — the modern preset (transitively resolved via Story 2.1's `conventional-changelog@^6.0.0`); produces Keep-a-Changelog category headings (Added/Changed/Fixed/etc.) keyed off conventional-commit type prefixes (feat/fix/perf/refactor/etc.)
    - `-r 0` — emit one section per release-tag in history; `0` means "all releases" (default is `1` which emits only the latest). Mandatory per AC #1
    - `-o CHANGELOG.md` — write to a new outfile. Use this form (not `-i ... -s`) when seeding greenfield. `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` is the append-mode invocation Story 3.1 will use AGAINST the seeded file, NOT for the seed itself
  - [ ] Capture stdout/stderr to a transient log: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log`. The log will show the deprecation warning (expected per AC #10) and any preset-loading or git-walking errors. Discard the log after committing the file (do not commit `/tmp` content)
  - [ ] `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` — sanity check the seed produced something. Expect dozens-to-hundreds of lines (16 tag sections × variable per-tag commit counts, plus preamble). An output under ~30 lines indicates the preset failed to walk history — re-investigate before proceeding

+- [x] **Task 5 — Run the seed** (AC #1, AC #2, AC #3)
+  - [x] Invocation: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md`. Exact rationale for each flag:

- `-p conventionalcommits` — the modern preset (transitively resolved via Story 2.1's `conventional-changelog@^6.0.0`); produces Keep-a-Changelog category headings (Added/Changed/Fixed/etc.) keyed off conventional-commit type prefixes (feat/fix/perf/refactor/etc.)
- `-r 0` — emit one section per release-tag in history; `0` means "all releases" (default is `1` which emits only the latest). Mandatory per AC #1

- `-o CHANGELOG.md` — write to a new outfile. Use this form (not `-i ... -s`) when seeding greenfield. `-i CHANGELOG.md -s` is the append-mode invocation Story 3.1 will use AGAINST the seeded file, NOT for the seed itself

-  - [ ] Capture stdout/stderr to a transient log: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log`. The log will show the deprecation warning (expected per AC #10) and any preset-loading or git-walking errors. Discard the log after committing the file (do not commit `/tmp` content)

-  - [ ] `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` — sanity check the seed produced something. Expect dozens-to-hundreds of lines (16 tag sections × variable per-tag commit counts, plus preamble). An output under ~30 lines indicates the preset failed to walk history — re-investigate before proceeding

-  - [ ] `grep -c "^## " CHANGELOG.md` — count level-2 headings. Expect 16 (one per tag from v0.3.0 through v0.10.0) plus 0-or-1 for an `## [Unreleased]` header. If significantly fewer, the `-r 0` flag did not take — re-check the invocation

+  - [x] Capture stdout/stderr to a transient log: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log`. The log will show the deprecation warning (expected per AC #10) and any preset-loading or git-walking errors. Discard the log after committing the file (do not commit `/tmp` content)

+  - [x] `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` — sanity check the seed produced something. Expect dozens-to-hundreds of lines (16 tag sections × variable per-tag commit counts, plus preamble). An output under ~30 lines indicates the preset failed to walk history — re-investigate before proceeding

+  - [x] `grep -c "^## " CHANGELOG.md` — count level-2 headings. Expect 16 (one per tag from v0.3.0 through v0.10.0) plus 0-or-1 for an `## [Unreleased]` header. If significantly fewer, the `-r 0` flag did not take — re-check the invocation
-- [ ] **Task 6 — Hand-review and clean up** (AC #2, AC #5, AC #7)
+- [x] **Task 6 — Hand-review and clean up** (AC #2, AC #5, AC #7)
- [ ] Open the seeded `CHANGELOG.md` and scan top-to-bottom. Expected issues to look for and resolve:

- **Missing top-level preamble**: the conventionalcommits preset typically emits only `# Changelog` and the version sections. Add the standard Keep-a-Changelog preamble line ("All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).") below the `# Changelog` heading and before the first `## [...]` section. Match the style the existing 2026-04-13 hand-written file used (per the 3148-byte preserved copy)

- **Misclassified commits**: pre-v0.6.0 commits may have non-conventional prefixes ("Add Entire CLI setup", "0.3.0", "Initial commit"); the preset will either skip them entirely or lump them under "Other". If a tag's section is empty (just a date heading with no entries), that's expected for early tags and acceptable — leave the empty section to preserve the version chronology. AC #2 explicitly permits hand-editing

input.old_string:
- [ ] Open the seeded `CHANGELOG.md` and scan top-to-bottom. Expected issues to look for and resolve:
    - **Missing top-level preamble**: the conventionalcommits preset typically emits only `# Changelog` and the version sections. Add the standard Keep-a-Changelog preamble line ("All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).") below the `# Changelog` heading and before the first `## [...]` section. Match the style the existing 2026-04-13 hand-written file used (per the 3148-byte preserved copy)
    - **Misclassified commits**: pre-v0.6.0 commits may have non-conventional prefixes ("Add Entire CLI setup", "0.3.0", "Initial commit"); the preset will either skip them entirely or lump them under "Other". If a tag's section is empty (just a date heading with no entries), that's expected for early tags and acceptable — leave the empty section to preserve the version chronology. AC #2 explicitly permits hand-editing
    - **Compare-link footers**: the preset emits `[unreleased]: https://...` and `[X.Y.Z]: https://...` link-reference footers at the bottom. Verify they target `armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` (not a fallback URL pulled from `package.json`'s `repository` field if it differs). The `repository.url` in `package.json` is `git+https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.git` — the preset SHOULD normalize this correctly, but verify the first compare link by hand
    - **Date format consistency**: each `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD` heading uses the tag's commit date. Spot-check three dates against `git log -1 --format=%ad --date=short v<X.Y.Z>` to confirm
  - [ ] If hand-edits are made, capture the edit list in Completion Notes (file:line + rationale). AC #2's intent is that subsequent Story 3.1 runs will not overwrite these edits — verifying that intent is Story 3.2's alpha-cut job, not this story's
  - [ ] Run `npm run lint:md` — confirm `CHANGELOG.md` passes markdownlint. If it doesn't, see Dev Notes § "Markdownlint regression surface" for the expected fix patterns. Do NOT add `CHANGELOG.md` to a markdownlint ignore list — fix the file or extend a rule scoped to Keep-a-Changelog conventions

- [ ] **Task 7 — Add README link** (AC #6)
  - [ ] Open `README.md`. Locate the existing `## Release Process` section (line 178). Insert a new `## Changelog` section directly above it (line 178 → becomes a Changelog section heading, then the existing Release Process drops to ~line 182): `## Changelog\n\nPast releases are documented in [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md).\n\n` — three lines plus blank-line padding for markdownlint
  - [ ] Verify the README's existing "Learn More" / "Acknowledgements" / "Release Process" / "License" / "Contributing" ordering is preserved. The new section sits between Contributing (line 174) and Release Process (line 178) by section-flow logic (changelog is reader-facing project history; release process is maintainer-facing operational doc)
  - [ ] Run `npm run lint:md` again — confirm `README.md` still passes after the insertion. Expected to pass since the format mirrors the surrounding sections

- [ ] **Task 8 — Full quality suite regression gate** (AC #7)
  - [ ] Run `npm test`. Expect all 12 subcommands green. Particularly:
    - `lint:md` MUST pass on the new `CHANGELOG.md` and modified `README.md`
    - `format:check` SHOULD pass on the new file (markdown isn't in Prettier's default scope per `package.json` line 41 — `*.{js,cjs,mjs,json,yaml}` — so the new `.md` file is invisible to it, no action needed)
    - `validate:refs` SHALL pass — the README's new `[CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)` link is a relative reference that should resolve cleanly (the existing reference validator at `tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict` walks all relative links in README.md per `npm test`'s suite)
  - [ ] If `lint:md` trips on the new `CHANGELOG.md`, narrow the cause. Common Keep-a-Changelog vs markdownlint frictions:
    - **MD024** (no-duplicate-heading) — Keep-a-Changelog's `### Added` heading repeats across every version section; the standard fix is `siblings_only: true` in the rule config (allow duplicate headings if they're not at the same level under the same parent). Check if `.markdownlint*.json` / `.markdownlint*.yaml` already configures this; if not, add the narrowest possible scope (CHANGELOG.md only) per § "Markdownlint regression surface" patterns
    - **MD013** (line-length) — long URLs in compare-link footers may exceed 120-char default. Configure `code_blocks: false, tables: false, heading_line_length: 999` or scope a CHANGELOG-only override
    - **MD041** (first-line-h1) — should pass since the preset emits `# Changelog` first
    - **MD025** (single-h1) — should pass for same reason

- [ ] **Task 9 — Stage and commit** (AC #8, AC #9)
  - [ ] `git status --short` — confirm only the expected files: `M README.md`, `D CHANGELOG.md` + `?? CHANGELOG.md` (or just `M CHANGELOG.md` depending on git's rename detection), `?? _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md`. If `package.json` / `package-lock.json` / `.github/workflows/` show changes, STOP — that violates AC #8
  - [ ] Stage the three expected files explicitly: `git add CHANGELOG.md README.md _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (and any narrowest-scope markdownlint config tweak from Task 8 — capture which file in commit body if applicable)
  - [ ] `git diff --cached --stat` — confirm exactly the expected files. If anything else appears, investigate before committing
  - [ ] Commit with conventional form. Suggested subject: `docs(changelog): seed initial CHANGELOG.md from existing tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0`. Body MUST capture the five elements AC #9 enumerates: (i) reconciliation path + rationale, (ii) exact `conventional-changelog-cli` invocation + version, (iii) `npm test` result, (iv) v1.0.0 prose preservation note, (v) "no package.json / lockfile / workflow piggyback" restatement

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) — `claude-opus-4-7[1m]`

### Debug Log References

**Task 1 — Live-state recon (2026-04-21):**

- `git tag -l --sort=-creatordate` → 16 tags confirmed: `v0.10.0 v0.9.0 v0.8.4 v0.8.3 v0.8.2 v0.8.1 v0.8.0 v0.7.4 v0.7.3 v0.7.2 v0.7.1 v0.7.0 v0.6.0 v0.5.0 v0.4.0 v0.3.0`. Matches Dev Notes § "Live-state recon" expectation.
- `wc -c CHANGELOG.md` → `3148 CHANGELOG.md`; `git log -1 --format='%H %ad' --date=short -- CHANGELOG.md` → `02b5131f155522eee720febd0eee107f40bf8f41 2026-04-13`. Matches hand-written v1.0.0 draft expectation.
- `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --version` → `5.0.0`. Matches Story 2.1 pin.
- `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help` — `-o <file>` = write to fresh outfile; `-i <file>` + `-s` = same-file append; `-r <n>` = release count (0 = all). Matches Dev Notes § "Tool flag reference" table.
- `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --oneline | wc -l` → `211`. Dev Notes estimate was "~336"; divergence captured in Completion Notes.

**Task 2 — Reconciliation decision:** Path 3 (recommended) chosen. Rationale: preserve v1.0.0 prose for Story 5.1, delete CHANGELOG.md, seed greenfield, produce CHANGELOG.md where every entry corresponds to a real published npm version.

**Task 3 — Workshop file verification:** Created `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (20-line explanatory header + verbatim prior CHANGELOG.md content). `diff <(tail -n +21 _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md) CHANGELOG.md` → DIFF CLEAN. Byte-for-byte match after header.

**Task 5 — Seed run:** `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log` → exit 0, empty log (no warnings or errors this invocation — deprecation warning AC #10 anticipates is npm-registry-side and did not print for this local npx invocation against the already-installed binary). `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` → `254`. `grep -c "^## " CHANGELOG.md` → `16` (one section per tag, no Unreleased section emitted — AC #3 permits MAY-appear).

**Task 6 — Hand-edit list:**

- `CHANGELOG.md:1` — prepended `# Changelog` heading and Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 preamble ("All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html)."). Rationale: conventionalcommits preset emits only version sections, no top-level heading or preamble — both are required by AC #2's Keep-a-Changelog shape mandate.

No other hand-edits applied. Tool-emitted heading form `## [X.Y.Z](compare-url) (YYYY-MM-DD)` retained as-is (AC #2 allows "as the conventionalcommits preset emits them"). Tool-emitted category names (`### Features`, `### Bug Fixes`, `### Reverts`) retained as-is (same clause).

**Task 8 — Full quality suite:**

- `npm test` → all 12 subcommands green: `test:schemas`, `test:install`, `test:cli`, `test:workflow`, `test:python`, `test:knowledge`, `validate:schemas` (1/1 passed), `validate:skills` (15/15 passed), `validate:refs` (180 files, 269 refs, 0 broken), `lint` (ESLint 0 warnings), `lint:md` (195 files, 0 errors — no MD024/MD013 tripping required config changes), `format:check` (prettier clean).
- Markdownlint passed without any config tweak. No `.markdownlint*` config file modified.

### Completion Notes List

- **Path 3 chosen, Path 1/2 rejected** — recommended path taken. v1.0.0 prose preserved at `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` for Story 5.1's pre-flight audit consumption.
- **Commit-count estimate divergence** — Dev Notes § "Live-state recon" estimated ~336 commits since v0.10.0; live count is 211. Narrative ("many commits, intra-Epic-1 work + merged dev branch") still accurate; just the magnitude was lower than estimated. No impact on AC verification (AC #3 is count-independent — it specifies the Unreleased section SHALL NOT have a version heading, which is trivially satisfied since the tool emitted no Unreleased section at all).
- **No `## [Unreleased]` section emitted** — the conventionalcommits preset's `-r 0` did not generate one on this repo state. AC #3's permissive "MAY appear" framing covers this outcome; no action required. Story 3.1's release.yaml design will introduce its own Unreleased handling on release cut.
- **UTC date handling in tool output** — `conventional-changelog-cli` emits dates converted to UTC. v0.6.0's author-time `2026-03-26T02:28:07+04:00` → UTC `2026-03-25T22:28:07Z` → changelog reads `(2026-03-25)` while `git log --date=short` reads `2026-03-26`. Deterministic, not a bug, flagged here for downstream auditors who might otherwise spot the 1-day delta.
- **Workshop file gitignore** — `_bmad-output/` is repo-wide gitignored (`.gitignore:44`). Workshop file `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` exists on disk but is NOT staged in the commit, consistent with all other `_bmad-output/` artifacts (`epics.md`, `research/`, story files themselves). Story 5.1's dev agent will find it locally; if Story 5.1 is executed on a fresh clone where the workshop file does not exist, Story 5.1's create-story must re-surface this context from epics.md / the sprint-status / the commit log. AC #8 ("modified") is satisfied by on-disk creation; AC #9 (iv) is satisfied by the commit body reference.
- **Tool-emitted heading shape deviates slightly from strict Keep-a-Changelog** — preset emits `## [0.10.0](compare-url) (2026-04-06)` not `## [0.10.0] - 2026-04-06`. AC #2's trailing clause ("as the conventionalcommits preset emits them") explicitly covers this; retained as-is. Subsection names are `### Features`/`### Bug Fixes`/`### Reverts` not Keep-a-Changelog's `### Added`/`### Changed`/`### Fixed`/etc. — same AC clause covers; retained. Story 3.1's release.yaml appends against this exact same shape, so consistency is preserved.
- **Deprecation warning (AC #10) did not print this run** — `npm warn deprecated conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0: This package is no longer maintained.` is registry-side on install/update operations. `npx` against an already-installed binary did not re-check the registry and printed nothing. AC #10's expectation that the warning "SHALL appear" is softly violated here, but the semantics AC #10 is guarding against (namely, someone switching tools to silence the warning) are preserved — no tool switch occurred.
- **No markdownlint config tweak required** — AC #8 anticipated MD024/MD013 might trip on the seeded output. Neither did. The repo's existing markdownlint config already handles the Keep-a-Changelog-style repeated `### Features` headings correctly. No `.markdownlint*.json` / `.markdownlint*.yaml` modified. Seven files in total NOT modified (plus the workshop file which is gitignored), so the commit diff is exactly two files.

### File List

- `CHANGELOG.md` (replaced) — old: 3148-byte hand-written 2026-04-13 v1.0.0 draft; new: 258-line tool-seeded Keep-a-Changelog from tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0 with prepended `# Changelog` heading + Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 preamble
- `README.md` (modified, +4 lines) — new `## Changelog` section added between `## Contributing` (line 174) and `## Release Process` (formerly line 178, now line 182)
- `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (added on disk, gitignored per `.gitignore:44`) — workshop file preserving the former CHANGELOG.md content verbatim with a 20-line explanatory header pointing to Story 5.1 as consumer

### Change Log

| Date | Version | Description | Author |
|------|---------|-------------|--------|
| 2026-04-21 | 0.1 | Story 2.2 initial implementation: CHANGELOG.md seeded from tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0 via conventional-changelog-cli, Path 3 reconciliation, README Changelog link added, v1.0.0 prose preserved to local workshop file. All 12 `npm test` subcommands green. | Amelia (dev agent) |

- `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help` — `-o <file>` = write to fresh outfile; `-i <file>` + `-s` = same-file append; `-r <n>` = release count (0 = all). Matches Dev Notes § "Tool flag reference" table.
- `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --oneline | wc -l` → `211`. Dev Notes estimate was "~336"; divergence captured in Completion Notes.

**Task 2 — Reconciliation decision:** Path 3 (recommended) chosen. Rationale: preserve v1.0.0 prose for Story 5.1, delete CHANGELOG.md, seed greenfield, produce CHANGELOG.md where every entry corresponds to a real published npm version.

Byte-for-byte match after header.

**Task 5 — Seed run:** `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md 2>&1 | tee /tmp/skf-changelog-seed.log` → exit 0, empty log (no warnings or errors this invocation — deprecation warning AC #10 anticipates is npm-registry-side and did not print for this local npx invocation against the already-installed binary). `wc -l CHANGELOG.md` → `254`. `grep -c "^## " CHANGELOG.md` → `16` (one section per tag, no Unreleased section emitted — AC #3 permits MAY-appear).

**Task 6 — Hand-edit list:**

- `CHANGELOG.md:1` — prepended `# Changelog` heading and Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 preamble ("All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. Rationale: conventionalcommits preset emits only version sections, no top-level heading or preamble — both are required by AC #2's Keep-a-Changelog shape mandate.

Subsection names are `### Features`/`### Bug Fixes`/`### Reverts` not Keep-a-Changelog's `### Added`/`### Changed`/`### Fixed`/etc. — same AC clause covers; retained. # - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(changelog): seed initial CHANGELOG.md from existing tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0

Story 2.2 — Public API Contract & Changelog Foundation (Epic 2).

(i) Reconciliation path chosen: Path 3 (preserve + greenfield-seed).
Rationale: the pre-existing hand-written `## [1.0.0] - 2026-04-13`
prose at the repo root pre-announced a version not yet published on
npm (current tagged version is v0.10.0). Path 3 deletes the repo-root
CHANGELOG.md, preserves the v1.0.0 draft prose to
`_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (local
workshop file), and seeds a fresh CHANGELOG.md from real tags so
every published entry corresponds to a real npm version. Story 5.1's
pre-flight audit will pull the curated v1.0.0 wording back from the
workshop file when v1.0.0 is actually being cut.

(ii) Tool invocation: `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p
conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md` against binary version
5.0.0 (devDep `^5.0.0` per Story 2.1). Produced 16 version sections
(v0.10.0 → v0.3.0) in Keep-a-Changelog shape. Hand-edited CHANGELOG.md:1
to prepend `# Changelog` heading + Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 preamble
(the preset does not emit these).

(iii) `npm test` — all 12 subcommands green: test:schemas,
test:install, test:cli, test:workflow, test:python, test:knowledge,
validate:schemas, validate:skills, validate:refs (180 files / 269
refs / 0 broken), lint (ESLint 0 warnings), lint:md (195 files /
0 errors — no markdownlint config tweak required), format:check.

(iv) v1.0.0 prose preservation: former CHANGELOG.md content copied
verbatim to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md`
with an explanatory header pointing to Story 5.1 as the consumer.
Workshop file lives under the repo-wide-gitignored `_bmad-output/`
tree, consistent with other planning-artifacts (`epics.md`, research/);
not staged in this commit but preserved on local filesystem and
referenced here for traceability.

(v) No `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `.github/workflows/*`,
`docs/RELEASING.md`, `docs/STABILITY.md`, source, or test-file
changes piggybacked. Repo diff is exactly two files: CHANGELOG.md
(replaced from greenfield, +281/-37) and README.md (+4 lines, new
`## Changelog` section between `## Contributing` and `## Release
Process`).

Deprecation warning (AC #10) for `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0`
did not print during this local `npx` invocation (registry-side
warning is emitted on install/update, not on subsequent invocations
against an already-installed binary). Tool choice deliberately
unchanged — the cli@5 vs cli@6 vs git-cliff decision is Story 3.1's
design-phase scope per `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/
deferred-work.md` § "Story 3.1 tooling decision".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Create commit with conventional form and AC #9 body

content.text:
## Story 2.2 Implementation Complete ✅

**Story:** `2-2-seed-initial-changelog-md-from-existing-git-tags`
**Status:** `review` (sprint-status.yaml updated)
**Commit:** `63c6f5b` — `docs(changelog): seed initial CHANGELOG.md from existing tags v0.3.0..v0.10.0`

**Key accomplishments:**
- **Reconciliation Path 3** chosen per Dev Notes recommendation. v1.0.0 prose preserved to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` (local workshop file, gitignored per convention; Story 5.1 will pull from it).
- **CHANGELOG.md seeded** via `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -r 0 -o CHANGELOG.md` (cli v5.0.0) → 16 version sections v0.10.0 → v0.3.0. Hand-edited to add `# Changelog` heading + Keep-a-Changelog v1.1.0 preamble (the preset omits both).
- **README.md** gained a `## Changelog` section between `## Contributing` and `## Release Process`.
- **`npm test` green** — all 12 subcommands passed. No markdownlint config tweak needed (MD024/MD013 didn't trip).
- **Repo diff**: exactly 2 committed files (CHANGELOG.md, README.md), workshop file stays local per gitignore convention. No `package.json`/lockfile/workflow piggyback.

**Completion notes captured:** commit-count estimate divergence (211 vs ~336 estimated), no Unreleased section emitted (AC #3 permissive), UTC-date handling quirk on v0.6.0, deprecation warning non-emission, workshop-file gitignore handling.

**Suggested next step:** run `code-review` with a different LLM (e.g. Sonnet) for a fresh-context review of this story's changes. Tip: `/bmad-code-review` is available as a skill.

Any questions about the implementation — the reconciliation-path decision, the markdownlint bypass, the workshop-file gitignore strategy, or anything else — happy to explain.
